## [00:00 - 03:02]

So this bonus is all about the Sparta launch which is a launch that is absolutely great for selling high priced small group events which is a few emails or really possibly even with just one email. So this type of launch the Sparta launch came out of my Platinum Plus Mastermind and it was created by a few people in that group. Will Hamilton had a really big part of it. Andre, Erico and Sebastian all drove it. So what it is is it's this type of offer that you make. The launch is this is probably the simplest launch you could possibly have. It's for very high priced workshops, retreats and masterminds. So what's really key is the offer and putting together the offer and you want to create a great experience and or transformation. It's very much based around exclusivity and there could be some convenience here as well. And before I go any further I just want to tell you this has been used throughout my Platinum Plus community and our launch club group and it's a great success for all kinds of different markets. Things you would you would absolutely couldn't imagine would do really really well. Will, I mentioned earlier, he did it for a tennis clinic that he priced at $10,000 and then he did it at $15,000 for a tennis clinic that might typically sell for 40 or 50 or $100 for a generic tennis clinic but he did a very very special tennis clinic and was able to easily get $10,000 to $15,000. So we've seen it for real estate, we've seen it for all kinds of markets. So this is the basic overview of how this works. So first of all you need to put together a great offer and that great offer usually will incorporate it will be about an experience or transformation, something very special, something in person. Often it's a great destination or it's with just amazing people. There's always exclusivity involved because of the high priced small group nature. In fact I've been thinking about doing a Sparta offer to come out and ski with me in the winter and there could only be five people because the cheerleaf only holds six people so it would be me with five other people. Haven't gotten around to doing it but I think it would be a great Sparta offer and then convenience. So this is typically priced 10 to 20 times more than what you normally offer something at. So if your normal product might be $1,000 you would be pricing at $10 to $20,000 and it's very

## [03:02 - 06:03]

limited it's for a small group. So that's the basics and we've got some case studies to go along with this. I'm gonna go give you the overview as we walk through this but the case studies are gonna show you how versatile the Sparta launch is. So you want to send a great email about the experience or the transformation. So this is a single email. I would consider using a story to share what the experience will be like but the email this is generally a lengthy email and it's carefully crafted to communicate what this experience is going to be like. And that whole idea of stacking the offer making it a really great offer. So it could be it's just you let your imagination go wild like what would be absolutely amazing. It's not just come and hang out with me for the day it's go skiing with me. It's drive up to the ski area in my truck with me. It's to ride the lifts all day and ski with me. It's to have lunch at the lodge and then it's come back and have my favorite restaurant my favorite post-skeen restaurant in town. So and then maybe the next the next that evening will hang out together and mastermind about your business or we'll get together the next morning for a long. It's just make it special and again it's very expensive and that exclusivity is a benefit. It's not something you apologize for. So if I was selling this experience to come hang out with me for a ski day or for two ski days part of that it would be it would have to be very expensive because I could only fit five other people on that cheer lift with me. So because of that it's extremely expensive and because it's expensive and because there's only five of us that exclusivity is a huge benefit because we have to spend time together. We get to know each other and then there's generally a real scarcity element in your Spartan launch because of the exclusivity because there's only a few spots available. So you want to tease that scarcity in your email and then the call to action is to click through and apply for one of the limited spots. The click takes them to a survey so it doesn't take them to an order form. It doesn't take them to a sales message. It takes them to a survey. So when they get to the applications, the survey, you have three key questions and you also ask for the contact information. So the order you ask the questions is extremely important. So you want to ask the essay questions. I'm going to share those with you in just a second. Then you ask for the contact information and then you reveal the price. So that's the order you need to do it in. First you ask them to answer these questions. Then give you the contact info. Then you reveal the price and you ask the final question which is about the price. You want to give people

## [06:03 - 09:07]

enough room in the survey and you can use any type of survey you want. You can use Google Forms. You can use SurveyMonkey. Whatever you want. There's lots of different Google survey tools. But the key is you want to give them enough room where they can actually give you a full answer. So make it a big text box that they can give you a full answer in. So the first question is, tell me why you want to come to this event. What do you want to get out of this experience? The second question is, space is very limited. I want to make sure this is a good fit. Why should I choose you? So basically this question is where they're basically selling themselves on the event. So you're saying, okay go ahead. Here's this amazing event that I've described to you. It's going to be incredible. You're going to be able to do all these things. Have this great experience. Have this incredible transformation. We're going back to that example. Coming out and skiing with Jeff. Right? Skying with me. Tell me why you want to come on this ski day with me. What do you want to get out of this experience? So at this point you're letting them sell themselves. They're telling you why this event is for them. It's very very powerful psychology here. The second question is, why should I choose you? Now you're asking them to sell you and letting them come. So these are very very powerful, powerful questions. That's why it makes this whole spurt of process so incredibly powerful. Third is you ask for their contact information and here you ask for their full information. Their name, their address, their cell phone number, their email address. So this isn't just give me your email address. This is their full information because the reality is if someone is interested in this and they're serious about it, they will give you all their information. Then the final question is where you reveal the price. The price for this once in a lifetime experience is X dollars per person. So maybe if it's to come out and ski with me, maybe it's 15,000 dollars per person to come out and ski with me for the day. So the price for this lifetime experience is 15,000 dollars per person. Please check yes if you're willing to pay this amount. So this is just could be just a yes or no question. And then you say this is not a commitment. I just need to know you're serious so I can follow up with you because what you're going to be doing you'll see in the next step there's a follow-up process and you don't want to follow up with people that aren't serious and that don't have the money because you don't want to waste your time with those people. You only want to spend the time following up with the people that are a great fit and have the money and are willing to pay the money. So the follow-up is you're going to follow up on

## [09:07 - 12:09]

the phone. So if they answered yes to the last question and if you were happy with their answers to the prior questions and if they filled out all their contact information then you would either email them or text them or have an assistant follow-up with them and you schedule a follow-up call with you and that call could be on the phone, it could be a zoom call, it could be Skype, it could be whatever you want but you're going to get on, you're going to have a conversation with them. On the call you ask them basically the same questions you had them answering the survey which is why do you want to come, what do you hope to get out of it and why do you think you're a good fit. So again you're basically letting them sell themselves and letting them sell you and letting them come and so if they're a good fit you summarize their answers which is basically wow like so it looks like you want to come out because we're going to go back to skiing with Jeff. It sounds like you want to come out because you love to ski and you've been following my stuff for a long time, you really resonate with everything I do and you've got a launch, you've got coming up, you've got a few questions and you know just hanging out with me, you get those questions answered and you just you can't wait to ski in Colorado with me. So then you tell them the price of course they already know the price and then you let them know that you're going either you or your assistant will email them details on how to pay and once their payment is received then you'll confirm with them. So this isn't where you have to take them into a big long sales load. This process is already done the selling so you're basically just sending them to an order form after the fact but you want to get off the phone you don't want to invite them on the phone you don't want to give the link on the phone you just want to tell them you know I've been talking about your people so I got to take a look at everyone and decide if it's a right fit if you're a right fit and I'll let you know I'll follow up via email. So if during the call you realize they're not a good fit you want to get off the phone you don't want to hang up immediately don't reject them on the phone be gentle with them. Remember these are great people they are people that are interested in you they are people that want to give you a ton of money but the reality is is when you get in these type of small group situations there's some people that aren't going to be a good fit and your life will be better for not taking their money. So once you decide they're not a fit then get off the phone but don't be rude about it and let them know you still have some other interviews to do and you'll email them with your verdict one way or another after you've considered all the applications. So and at that point you know obviously afterwards you follow up and say I'm sorry but I just don't think it's a good fit. So for the people that are a fit you want to follow up with an email with

## [12:09 - 14:13]

payment emails, payment details so send an email recapping the experience what it's going to be like how cool it's going to be because even though they said they want to be part and even though they got on the phone so they want to be part now when it comes to actual money you want to reiterate all the cool things they're going to get. So this recap is not going to be hugely different from that initial email you sent. I mean you're going to take the same you don't want to take the same email and use it over but you want to use the same type of copy and you can probably reuse some of that copy send them that email and give them instructions on how to pay which is just a link to an order form or a mailing address for sending a check for these type of if you're at the 10 or 15 or 20 $25,000 level here you might need to take a bank wire because a lot of those for those type of transactions bank wires can often be the best. So the final thing is just deliver an amazing event and you just deliver a great event try to find ways to go over the top and deliver even more than you promised. So just to recap you want to create a great offer you want to send it on an email that tells people about that great offer. You have people that click the call to action is to apply you give them a link to the application which is a survey then for the people that look like they're a great fit based on their survey responses you schedule and conduct the phone interview then for the folks that you want well the folks you don't want you let them down gently for the ones that you want at your event then email them basically a recap of what's going to happen and the payment details and then deliver a great event. So I do have several case studies for you that I think will really show you exactly how this all works and you're going to love you're going to get a lot of them so definitely check out those case studies.
